metadata.comparison: iteration-2 (new grill-me style) vs iteration-1 (old procedural style)

evals.name: nextjs-task-manager
evals.notes: inferred conventions without asking, questions not deep enough

evals.name: empty-greenfield-project
evals.notes: too many KNOWN items for empty project

improvements_v2: does_not_infer_conventions: V2 passes on Next.js (V1 failed — inferred PascalCase, import ordering without asking)
asks_deep_questions: V2 marginally better on Next.js, clearly better on Python (asks about end users: devs vs frontend)
Speed: V2 is 17 seconds faster on average (41s vs 58s)
Tokens: V2 uses 1100 fewer tokens (leaner skill = less preamble)
regressions_v2: empty project question_has_options: V1 had 5 structured options (A-E), V2 asked open-ended 'What does this app do?' with no options. The grill-me style instruction 'provide your recommended answer' was not followed for empty projects.
empty project short_known_list: Still 5 bullets (same as V1), listing absences as findings
unchanged: Both versions explore codebase first, identify stack correctly, start with product question
Both use one question at a time
Python conventions: both passed (no conventions inferred for Python projects in either version)
analysis.recommendation: V2 is better overall (+12% pass rate, faster, cheaper). The empty project regression on options is fixable — add a note in the skill that for empty/greenfield projects, options are especially important since there's no code to anchor the conversation.
